subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Sustainability is placed at the centre of the investigation, with every analytical section returning to environmental, social and economic implications
Triple Bottom Line and Appleâspecific evidence are fully integrated throughout, driving both choice of tools and evaluative commentary
weaknesses: Operational definition of sustainability is delayed until later in the introduction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates excellent understanding and fully integrates the key concept of sustainability across the entire IA, from framing the research question to driving analysis and conclusions. Although a brief operational definition could strengthen clarity if introduced earlier, the depth and consistency of conceptual integration meet the topâband descriptor.

strengths: Supporting documents include a diverse mix of academic, industry and company sources that enhance depth of analysis
weaknesses: Listing thirteen documents exceeds the IB guideline of 3â5, automatically placing the work in the lowest band for number of documents
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., URL line breaks)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite the high relevance and depth of individual sources, the submission of more than five documents violates the IB requirement and restricts the criterion to band 1. The penalty for number of documents is nonânegotiable according to the rubric.

strengths: Clear and systematic application of the Triple Bottom Line and stakeholder frameworks to guide analysis
Creative linkage of afterâsales service to the âProcessesâ element of the marketing mix
weaknesses: Selection of tools is adequate but could be broadened with financial or riskâassessment models
Application would benefit from quantitative metrics (e.g., profitâmargin ratios) to deepen financial insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA employs relevant tools in a coherent way, satisfying the descriptor for band 3. While application is effective overall, limited selection and quantification prevent it from fully achieving band 4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective selection and use of quantitative and qualitative data linked directly to Appleâs PLE initiatives
Thorough evaluative commentary, including critical discussion of greenwashing and stakeholder tradeâoffs
weaknesses: No quantitative data on supplier program adoption rates
Financial depth could be enhanced with more detailed ratio analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis goes beyond description and uses a sufficient range of data to produce mostly effective, integrated evaluation. Minor gaps in supplier figures and financial depth keep the work in band 4 rather than band 5.

strengths: Conclusion synthesises stakeholder findings clearly and mirrors evidence presented
Explicitly answers the research question, showing full consistency with the preceding analysis
weaknesses: Introduces a new concept (subscription models) in the conclusion, which is not supported by prior analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are fully consistent with evidence and directly address the research question, fitting the top band. The only drawback is the introduction of unsupported ideas in the closing paragraph.

strengths: Logical progression with clear headings and coherent structure
Research question is clearly stated and guides the reportâs organization
weaknesses: Table of contents is cluttered and misaligned
Heading hierarchy is inconsistent in places, affecting readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates an appropriate, coherent structure throughout, meriting the highest band. Cosmetic issues in the table of contents and heading levels do not undermine overall organization.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (title page, TOC, citations, works cited)
Formatting meets IB requirements for inâtext and endâofâtext citations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully satisfies IB requirements, earning the top band. Minor grammatical and sentenceâstructure issues are noted but do not detract from overall compliance.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The report follows a logical progression (introduction, findings, stakeholder analysis, conclusion, works cited) and contains headings that guide the reader.  
â¢ Although the table of contents is cluttered, the overall structure remains coherent and appropriate, fulfilling the criteria for band 2.

Sources include Apple annual reports, academic journals, industry news articles and independent case studies, all directly linked to the arguments made.  
â¢ Citations are used to supply quantitative data (e.g., 12.8 million refurbished devices, 40 % resale-value premium) and qualitative insights (Right-to-Repair critique).  
â¢ The collection is varied and detailed, clearly informing multiple perspectives; this aligns with the descriptor ârelevant, in-depth and provide range of ideas,â meriting band 4.

The IA includes a title page, table of contents, correctly formatted in-text citations and a works-cited list, meeting IB presentation requirements.  
â¢ Hence all required elements are present, earning the top band for this strand.
Thirteen supporting documents are listed. The rubric stipulates that providing **more than five documents places the work in band 1** for this strand, irrespective of their quality.

TBL and stakeholder analysis are systematically applied to each group, and the discussion explicitly links findings back to the research question.  
â¢ The frameworks guide evaluation of people/planet/profit trade-offs and reveal tensions (e.g., high repair fees vs sustainability claims), showing **effective and relevant application**.  
â¢ Minor gaps (limited financial quantification) prevent a flawless application, but overall the work meets the âclear relevance and effective applicationâ standard for band 4.

The IA draws on multiple quantitative indicatorsârepair-rate decline, trade-in volumes, size of repair network, resale-value differentialsâand qualitative evidence from reports.  
â¢ Data are mostly current and directly linked to Appleâs sustainability initiatives, representing a **sufficient range** rather than isolated figures.  
â¢ This aligns with the band 4 descriptor of âsufficient selection and use of data.â

Analysis moves beyond description: it explains how PLE affects loyalty, margins, supplier costs and shareholder risk, and weighs counter-arguments such as accusations of greenwashing.  
â¢ Links between evidence and evaluation are generally clear, though financial depth could be stronger.  
â¢ The work therefore demonstrates âmostly effective analysis with some integration,â fitting band 4.

The final paragraph explicitly answers the research questionââPLE modelsâ¦ positively impact stakeholder satisfaction to a very high extentââafter weighing benefits and drawbacks.  
â¢ This clear, direct response matches the descriptor for band 3, the top mark available.

The student selects stakeholder analysis and the Triple Bottom Line as primary frameworks and briefly references the marketing-mix âprocessâ element.  
â¢ While these tools are appropriate and aligned to the sustainability focus, the selection is **adequate rather than extensive**; additional strategic tools (e.g., cost-benefit, financial ratios) could have enriched the investigation.  
â¢ This matches the band 3 descriptor of âadequate selection of tools and theories.â

The conclusion synthesises findings for customers, suppliers and shareholders and restates how PLE models influence each TBL pillar, mirroring the evidence presented.  
â¢ No new, unsupported claims are introduced; therefore conclusions are **fully consistent with the preceding analysis**, satisfying the highest band for this strand.

Apple-specific evidence is woven into almost every paragraph: Self-Service Repair, trade-in figures, number of technicians, supplier development fund, repair-rate statistics and refurbished-device volumes.  
â¢ The analysis consistently shows how each sustainability initiative affects Appleâs customers, suppliers and shareholders; it is not a generic discussion of circular models.  
â¢ Because this **company-level evidence is integrated from opening to conclusion** and used to drive evaluation, the work meets the description of âcomprehensive analysis fully integrated throughout,â warranting the top band.

The student places sustainability at the centre of the report: the research question is framed around the concept, the introduction defines it, and every analytical section repeatedly returns to the environmental, social and economic implications of Appleâs product-life-extension activities.  
â¢ Throughout the stakeholder analysis, each point is explicitly linked back to the three pillars of the Triple Bottom Line, demonstrating conceptual understanding rather than merely mentioning the term.  
â¢ Because references to sustainability drive the choice of tools (TBL) and the evaluative commentary, the concept is **fully integrated across the IA**, matching the descriptor for the highest band.

annotations.comment: New concepts (e.g., subscription models) appear in conclusion; avoid introducing fresh analysis here.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, providing a strong foundation for the investigation.
Key concept is identified but not defined until later; consider including a brief operational definition here to strengthen clarity.
The table of contents is overly dense and misaligned; simplify entries and align page numbers for improved readability.
Introduction effectively contextualizes Appleâs sustainability pledge and explains the CBM focus.
Acknowledgement of source bias and use of external reports shows critical evaluation of data reliability.
Limitation notes qualitative bias but could propose specific quantitative metrics to assess financial impact.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Good use of TBL definition and stakeholder frameworks to frame analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Sentence fragment (âAlongside increasing their products' repairability.â) interrupts flow; revise for grammatical completeness.
Quantitative data on repair network expansion is well integrated to demonstrate scope of PLE initiatives.
Consider adding a visual summary (table or chart) of PLE data to enhance clarity and support analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Use of customer repurchase rate (92%) effectively supports claims of loyalty.
Linking after-sales service to the âProcessesâ element of marketing mix shows creative application of theory.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Financial impacts of PLE are discussed qualitatively; include profit margin ratios or cost-benefit calculations to deepen insight.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Critical evaluation of high repair fees shows nuanced understanding of PLE trade-offs.
Several sentences are punctuated as fragments, which disrupts readability; proofread and consolidate.
Supplier analysis links TBL pillars to supply chain standards, showing clear relevance.
No quantitative figures on supplier program adoption; including uptake rates would strengthen data selection.
Consider applying risk assessment tools (e.g., SWOT or PESTEL) to evaluate supplier vulnerabilities.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Shareholder section integrates revenue and environmental data effectively to show PLE impact.
Heading hierarchy varies (h3 under h4); ensure consistent heading levels for clarity.
Incorporate financial trend charts or ratio analysis to quantify revenue shifts under PLE.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Conclusion succinctly answers the research question and synthesizes stakeholder impacts.
Refine the conclusion to focus on key findings and implications, reserving new ideas for future research.
Limitation paragraph acknowledges scope but could specify methods for future research to guide study extension.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Thirteen supporting documents exceed recommended range; trim to 3â5 highest-relevance sources.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., URL line breaks); apply uniform style guidelines.
Supporting documents include diverse academic, industry, and company sources demonstrating depth.
